title.en: EARTH OR ROCK DRILLING; OBTAINING OIL, GAS, WATER, SOLUBLE OR MELTABLE MATERIALS OR A SLURRY OF MINERALS FROM WELLS
Percussion drilling
Surface drives for drop hammers {or percussion drilling}, e.g. with a cable
with a reciprocating impulse member (E21B1/02, E21B1/38 take precedence)
Tool-carrier piston type, i.e. in which the tool is connected to an impulse member
Hammer piston type, i.e. in which the tool bit or anvil is hit by an impulse member

Surface drives for rotary drilling

{Mechanical motion converting means, e.g. reduction gearings (E21B4/10 takes precedence)}

Electric drives (E21B4/12 takes precedence)

combined with surface drive (E21B4/10 takes precedence)
Drives for drilling with combined rotary and percussive action
the rotation being continuous
the rotation being intermittent, e.g. obtained by ratchet device

Underwater drilling (using heave compensators E21B19/09)
Drilling by use of heat, e.g. flame drilling
Applying separate balls or pellets by the pressure of the drill, so-called shot-drilling
Drilling by liquid or gas jets, with or without entrained pellets (E21B7/14 takes precedence)
Driving or forcing casings or pipes into boreholes, e.g. sinking; Simultaneously drilling and casing boreholes

Drilling without earth removal, e.g. with self-propelled burrowing devices (E21B7/30 takes precedence)
Enlarging drilled holes, e.g. by counterboring

Percussion drill bits ({with helical conveying portion E21B10/445;} characterised by wear resisting parts E21B10/46)

Rotary drag type drill bits with teeth, blades or like cutting elements, e.g. fork-type bits, fish tail bits (characterised by wear resisting parts E21B10/46; by conduits or nozzles for drilling fluid E21B10/60; by detachable or adjustable parts E21B10/62)
Bits with helical conveying portion, e.g. screw type bits; Augers with leading portion or with detachable parts (E21B10/42 takes precedence)
characterised by wear resisting parts, e.g. diamond inserts
characterised by conduits or nozzles for drilling fluids (for roller bits E21B10/18; for percussion drill bits E21B10/38)

characterised by the whole or part thereof being insertable into or removable from the borehole without withdrawing the drilling pipe

for anchoring the tools or the like (E21B23/02 - E21B23/06 take precedence; anchoring of drives in the borehole E21B4/18)

operated by fluid means, e.g. actuated by explosion (E21B23/08 takes precedence)
for setting packers

for obtaining oriented cores
the core receiver being specially adapted for operation under water

Automatic control of the tool feed ({E21B44/005,} E21B44/10 take precedence)

for underwater installation
by visual inspection

Monitoring of down-hole pump systems, e.g. for the detection of "pumped-off" conditions

Determining slope or direction
Measuring depth or liquid level

Measuring diameters or related dimensions at the borehole
Locating or determining the position of objects in boreholes or wells {, e.g. the position of an extending arm}; Identifying the free or blocked portions of pipes
Locating fluid leaks, intrusions or movements
